title: Kallstroemia bicolor
taxonomy: Kallstroemia bicolor is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Zygophyllaceae. This plant is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The genus Kallstroemia is a group of flowering plants that are commonly known as caltrops. The Zygophyllaceae family is a group of flowering plants that are also known as jujube or creosote bushes. Kallstroemia bicolor is an annual or perennial herbaceous plant that grows in dry or sandy areas.

intro: Kallstroemia bicolor, also known as the desert bird's eye or Mexican flameflower, is a flowering plant species that belongs to the mallow family (Malvaceae). The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its various health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties, which can help to treat wounds, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Kallstroemia bicolor include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
